* **[INT. ABANDONED CARNIVAL – NIGHT]**  
The scene opens with a green mist swirling ominously around a rusted carousel. A tiny green car with a clown face peels out of nowhere, screeching to a halt. A ghostly figure in a clown costume, Zombo, steps out, cackling. “I am Zombo, the devil of Jipner’s Joyland! I shall haunt all who dare to ride Monster Mountain!” he booms. Then, he spots a man in a turnip costume sobbing on the ground. “Why, it’s Jolly Jipner! The turnip mascot who got replaced by a *real* clown!” Zombo sneers. Jolly wails, “I didn’t want to be replaced by a *clown*! I wanted to be replaced by a *celebrity*! Like *Tom Cruise*!” Zombo growls, “You’re a turnip, Jolly. You’re not even *fun*.”

* **[INT. JIPNER’S JOYLAND – PARK ENTRANCE – DAY]**  
The gang arrives, only to find the gates locked with a sign that says “CLOSED DUE TO GHOSTLY INFESTATION.” Fred reads the National Exaggerator, which claims the park is a secret HQ for cotton candy creatures. “That’s not even a thing,” Velma says. “It’s a *metaphor*, Velma,” Fred says. “Wait, no, that’s not even a *metaphor*,” Daphne says. Shaggy and Scooby, still in their cereal-induced hyper mode, are already trying to sneak in. “But Daphne, Monster Mountain is *literally* the most fun thing ever!” Shaggy whines. “Unless it’s haunted by a *clown ghost*,” Scooby adds. “That’s not even a *thing* either,” Velma says. “It’s a *thing* now,” Fred says, “because *Red Herring* did it.”
